ops.title: Live CDP bridge (Touch-ID-gated, host/browser-routed)
ops.description:
Umbrella epic for the live CDP bridge feature. Full plan: ~/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-01/plans/cookiesync-should-also-expose-glittery-avalanche.md. Integration anchors: see note "CDP-bridge integration surface (post-v0.12.0 auth refactor)".

Phase A (local): launch throwaway Chrome with --remote-debugging-pipe on isolated 0700 user-data-dir; full-profile decrypt (SeedState) + CDP Storage.setCookies seeding preserving CHIPS/host-only/prefix/SameSite; token-gated single-client WS broker (coder/websocket) owning the pipe; dedicated strict-biometric bridge consent on auth.Broker separate from cookie grants; two-secret capability model; session registry + reaper + crash-durable liveness-pipe supervisor; `cookiesync bridge open/ls/stop` CLI.
Phase B (cross-host): remoteBridgeOpen ssh shell-out (host-identity auth), unified routed consent for the seed tap, detached `ssh -L` tunnel under context.WithoutCancel, distributed lease with peer self-reap, tunnel-drop terminal.
Security bar: codex audit closed 4 Criticals — no raw CDP port (pipe not port), strict biometrics (no passcode/bareOutcomes fallback), warm cookie grant can't open a bridge, capability required for management ops.

ops.text: go build/vet/test ./... green with the bridge package + auth/daemon/cli/helper wiring
Local: cookiesync bridge open drives a login-gated page via agent-browser connect, with a strict Touch ID tap (passcode rejected) and no raw CDP port listening (lsof)
Cross-host: bridge open --host <peer> routes the tap to the peer and drives over the ssh -L tunnel; killing the origin daemon leaves no orphaned browser on the peer
codex security re-audit confirms the 4 Criticals stay closed on the final diff
